parsing_res_list.block_content:
Vectors whose inner p product is zero are called orthogonal.The length or norm of a vector in V is defined by (2)$\left\|\boldsymbol{a}\right\|=\sqrt{(\boldsymbol{a},\boldsymbol{a})}\quad(\geq0).$ 
A vector of norm   a unit vector. From these axioms and from (2)one can derive the basic cinequality (3)$\left\|\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b}\right\|\leq\left\|\mathbf{a}\right\|\left\|\mathbf{b}\right\|$ (Cauchy - Schwarz inequality)From t this follows 
(4)$\left\|\boldsymbol{a}+\boldsymbol{b}\right\|\leq\left\|\boldsymbol{a}\right\|+\left\|\boldsymbol{b}\right\|$ (Triangle inequality).A simple direct calculation gives (5)$\left\|\boldsymbol{a}+\boldsymbol{b}\right\|^2+\left\|\boldsymbol{a}-\boldsymbol{b}\right\|^2=2\left(\left\|\boldsymbol{a}\right\|^2+\left\|\boldsymbol{b}\right\|^2\right)$ (Parallelogram  equality).
Advanced Engineering Mathematics, 10/e by Edwin Kreyszig Copyright 2011 by John Wiley & Sons. All rights reserved.

core_content.markdown:
Vectors whose inner p product is zero are called orthogonal.The length or norm of a vector in V is defined by (2)$\left\|\boldsymbol{a}\right\|=\sqrt{(\boldsymbol{a},\boldsymbol{a})}\quad(\geq0).$ 

A vector of norm   a unit vector. From these axioms and from (2)one can derive the basic cinequality (3)$\left\|\mathbf{a},\mathbf{b}\right\|\leq\left\|\mathbf{a}\right\|\left\|\mathbf{b}\right\|$ (Cauchy - Schwarz inequality)From t this follows 

(4)$\left\|\boldsymbol{a}+\boldsymbol{b}\right\|\leq\left\|\boldsymbol{a}\right\|+\left\|\boldsymbol{b}\right\|$ (Triangle inequality).A simple direct calculation gives (5)$\left\|\boldsymbol{a}+\boldsymbol{b}\right\|^2+\left\|\boldsymbol{a}-\boldsymbol{b}\right\|^2=2\left(\left\|\boldsymbol{a}\right\|^2+\left\|\boldsymbol{b}\right\|^2\right)$ (Parallelogram  equality).
